equipment.description: Primary feed pump for crude distillation unit
Standby feed pump for crude distillation unit

failure_modes.name: Mechanical Seal Leak
failure_modes.description: Leakage through pump mechanical seal faces due to wear, contamination, or improper lubrication

failure_modes.name: Bearing Failure
failure_modes.description: Degradation of rolling or sleeve bearings due to fatigue, contamination, or inadequate lubrication

failure_modes.name: Tube Fouling
failure_modes.description: Accumulation of deposits on heat exchanger tubes reducing heat transfer efficiency

failure_modes.name: Corrosion Under Insulation (CUI)
failure_modes.description: External corrosion occurring beneath thermal insulation due to moisture ingress

failure_modes.name: Excessive Vibration
failure_modes.description: Abnormal vibration levels indicating misalignment, imbalance, or structural issues

failure_modes.name: Valve Passing
failure_modes.description: Internal leakage through valve seat preventing complete shutoff

regulatory_references.title: Inspection and Safe Practices for Refineries
regulatory_references.requirement: All rotating equipment with mechanical seals handling hazardous fluids shall undergo seal inspection during every planned turnaround or at intervals not exceeding 24 months

regulatory_references.title: Inspection and Safe Practices for Refineries
regulatory_references.requirement: Thickness measurements on pressure vessels shall be documented and trended. Any wall loss exceeding 10% of nominal thickness requires engineering assessment within 30 days

regulatory_references.title: Chapter IV - Safety
regulatory_references.requirement: Pressure vessels and equipment shall be examined by a competent person at least once every 12 months, with records maintained for inspection

regulatory_references.title: Static and Mobile Pressure Vessels Rules
regulatory_references.requirement: Every pressure vessel shall display a valid fitness certificate issued by authorized inspector with validity not exceeding 2 years

regulatory_references.title: Lockout-Tagout Procedures
regulatory_references.requirement: LOTO procedures shall include verification of zero energy state by attempting equipment startup before maintenance commences

regulatory_references.title: Guidelines on Permit to Work System
regulatory_references.requirement: All hot work permits shall specify fire watch requirements and duration, with minimum 30-minute fire watch after completion of hot work

regulatory_references.title: Hazardous Area Classification
regulatory_references.requirement: Equipment installed in Zone 1 or Zone 2 areas shall be certified for the appropriate hazardous area classification

regulatory_references.title: Layout for Oil and Gas Installations
regulatory_references.requirement: Minimum separation distances between storage tanks shall be maintained as per tank diameter requirements

regulatory_references.title: Steel for General Structural Purposes
regulatory_references.requirement: All structural steel used in process equipment supports shall have mill test certificates traceable to material specifications

regulatory_references.title: Safe Handling of Hazardous Chemicals
regulatory_references.requirement: Lubricants and chemicals used in rotating equipment shall be approved for the service conditions and documented in equipment records

lubricants.name: EconoLube Standard 320
lubricants.notes: Not approved for high-criticality pumps due to incompatibility with fluorocarbon seals
